CHINA CLAIMS BREAKTHROUGH IN ‘ARTIFICIAL SUN’ PROGRAM

China says its next-gen fusion reactor HL-3 is moving toward industrialisation, with nuclear fusion named a key priority in its 15th Five-Year Plan (2026–30).

According to Chinese state media:
-  HL-3 has achieved temperatures exceeding 100 million°C
- Key components like the “first wall” now fully indigenised
- Fusion ignition test targeted around 2027
- Roadmap aims for a pilot reactor by ~2035 and demo reactor by ~2045

China claims the project brings it closer to controlled nuclear fusion, seen as a potential source of clean, near-limitless energy.

RT by @OsintTV: Public anger erupted late at night across Khyber district due to Pakistan Armed Forces killing its own Citizens at Khyber District in the Drone strike.

Protesters, including workers of Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) and local civil society groups, poured onto the streets demanding accountability. Elected representatives Abdul Ghani Afridi and Iqbal Afridi joined the protests, sitting on roads in defiance, while Pashtun leader Khan Wali Afridi led demonstrations outside the Frontier Corps Teaching Hospital.

The message from the ground was unambiguous. Protesters openly equated the military’s actions with terrorism, raising slogans: “Yeh jo dehshatgardi hai, iske peeche wardi hai.”
